steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure needed
steps.content: After “investigate how,” the blank must contain a noun clause—specifically, an indirect question—that explains what the team is investigating. An indirect question is a subordinate clause that acts as the object of a verb, not a direct question or independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the indirect question rule
steps.content: Indirect questions require statement word order (subject before auxiliary verb) and a period, not a question mark. Choice B uses statement word order with a period correctly (“their use could be better regulated.”), while the others either reverse the word order or use improper punctuation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
